title: DB migration: add last_respawn_at to persistent_agents
spec:
Add a nullable timestamp column `last_respawn_at` to the `persistent_agents` table.

Create a new migration file in the migrations directory (follow the existing naming convention). The migration should:
- Add column: `last_respawn_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL` to `persistent_agents`
- Include an appropriate rollback (DROP COLUMN)

Do not modify any TypeScript files — this job is migration-only. The column will be written by the auto-respawn logic added in a later job.
acceptance_tests:
Given the migration has been applied
When I inspect the persistent_agents table schema
Then a nullable timestamptz column named last_respawn_at exists
And the column defaults to NULL for existing rows

Given the migration has been applied
When I run the rollback
Then the last_respawn_at column is removed without error

title: Extend PersistentAgent interface with respawn state fields
spec:
Add respawn circuit-breaker tracking fields to the PersistentAgent interface (in `packages/shared/src/types.ts` or wherever the PersistentAgent interface is defined — search for it).

Fields to add:
- `respawnFailureCount: number` — tracks consecutive respawn failures; initialize to 0 when the agent is created
- `lastRespawnFailureAt: number | null` — unix timestamp of the most recent respawn failure; initialize to null

Also ensure the in-memory PersistentAgent objects created in `handlePersistentJob()` (executor.ts ~line 2170) initialize these two new fields with their zero values.

Do not add any logic yet — this job only adds the type shape and initialization.
acceptance_tests:
Given the PersistentAgent interface
When I inspect its type definition
Then it includes respawnFailureCount: number
And it includes lastRespawnFailureAt: number | null

Given a new persistent agent is spawned via handlePersistentJob
When the agent object is created in memory
Then respawnFailureCount is initialized to 0
And lastRespawnFailureAt is initialized to null

Given the TypeScript project compiles
When the new fields are added
Then there are no type errors in the build

title: Gap 1: Liveness check in persistent agent heartbeat
spec:
Modify the heartbeat `setInterval` block inside `handlePersistentJob()` in `packages/local-agent/src/executor.ts` (~line 2088).

Changes:
1. At the very top of each heartbeat tick body, before any `capturePane()` call, call `isTmuxSessionAlive(persistentAgent.tmuxSession)`. `isTmuxSessionAlive` already exists at ~line 3918 and is used for regular jobs in `pollJob()`.
2. If the session is dead:
   - Log a structured warning: `[executor] Persistent agent session dead: role=${role}, session=${session}`
   - `return` / `continue` to skip the rest of the heartbeat body for this tick
   - (Respawn delegation will be wired in a follow-up job; for now just skip)
3. Narrow the existing catch block that wraps `capturePane()`: after the liveness check is in place, errors reaching the catch are genuinely unexpected. Change the catch from `console.warn('Failed to capture pane...')` to `console.error('[executor] Unexpected capturePane error: ...')` with the error included.

Do not add respawn logic yet — this job is detection and logging only.
acceptance_tests:
Given a persistent agent is running with an active tmux session
When the heartbeat ticks
Then isTmuxSessionAlive is called before capturePane
And no warning is logged for a healthy session

Given a persistent agent whose tmux session has been killed (tmux kill-session -t <session>)
When the next heartbeat tick fires
Then the log contains: [executor] Persistent agent session dead: role=<role>, session=<session>
And capturePane is NOT called for that tick
And no unhandled exception is thrown

Given a healthy session where capturePane throws an unexpected error
When the catch block fires
Then the error is logged at error level (not warn)
And the error object is included in the log output

title: Gap 2: Post-spawn health check after spawnTmuxSession
spec:
In `handlePersistentJob()` in `packages/local-agent/src/executor.ts`, add an inline health check immediately after the `spawnTmuxSession()` call (~line 2050).

Steps to implement:
1. After `spawnTmuxSession()` returns, wait 2000ms (Claude Code takes ~1s to begin initializing).
2. Call `isTmuxSessionAlive(sessionName)`. If false, the spawn failed immediately.
3. If alive, additionally check the pane exit status via: `tmux display-message -p -t <session> "#{pane_dead_status}"`. A non-zero value means the pane process has already exited with an error.
4. If dead or non-zero exit: tail the log file (`~/.zazigv2/logs/{jobId}.log`, which already exists via `startPipePane()`) to capture early stderr output — read up to the first 50 lines. Include this in the failure log.
5. Log the failure with structured context: session name, exit code (if available), and the captured log snippet.
6. Throw an error (or return an error result matching the existing error-return pattern in `handlePersistentJob`) so the caller can handle it.

The spawn function signature does NOT change — everything lives inline in `handlePersistentJob()`.
acceptance_tests:
Given spawnTmuxSession is called with a valid session
When the spawned pane remains alive after 2 seconds
Then the health check passes with no error thrown
And execution continues normally

Given spawnTmuxSession is called but the pane immediately exits (simulated with a bad command)
When 2 seconds have elapsed
Then isTmuxSessionAlive returns false
And the log contains structured failure context including session name and log snippet
And an error is thrown or an error result is returned to the caller

Given spawnTmuxSession spawns a pane that exits with a non-zero code within 2 seconds
When the health check runs
Then pane_dead_status is read via tmux display-message
And the non-zero exit code appears in the failure log

title: Gap 3: Auto-respawn method with circuit breaker
spec:
Add a private method `respawnPersistentAgentIfDead()` to the executor class in `packages/local-agent/src/executor.ts`, and wire it into both the heartbeat liveness check (Gap 1) and the post-spawn failure handler (Gap 2).

**Method signature:**
```ts
private async respawnPersistentAgentIfDead(
  persistentAgent: PersistentAgent,
  job: Job,
  companyId: string,
  reason: 'heartbeat_detected_dead' | 'post_spawn_failed'
): Promise<void>
```

**Method body:**
1. Check `persistentAgent.resetInProgress` — if true, log and return immediately (prevents concurrent respawns).
2. Apply circuit breaker using existing constants `RESET_FAILURE_WINDOW_MS` (10 min) and `MAX_RESET_FAILURES` (3):
   - If `respawnFailureCount >= MAX_RESET_FAILURES` AND `lastRespawnFailureAt` is within `RESET_FAILURE_WINDOW_MS`: circuit is open — do NOT respawn.
   - If window has expired, reset `respawnFailureCount` to 0 before proceeding.
3. If circuit is open:
   - Set `persistent_agents.status = 'crashed'` in the DB.
   - Log a critical error with role, session, failure count.
   - Return without respawning.
4. If circuit is closed: call `reloadPersistentAgent(job, companyId)` (already exists ~line 1245).
   - On success: update `persistent_agents.last_respawn_at = NOW()` in the DB.
   - On failure: increment `persistentAgent.respawnFailureCount`, set `persistentAgent.lastRespawnFailureAt = Date.now()`.
5. Log each attempt with structured fields: `role`, `reason`, `attempt` (= respawnFailureCount + 1), `sessionName`.

**Wire-up:**
- In the heartbeat tick (Gap 1): after logging the dead-session warning, call `await this.respawnPersistentAgentIfDead(persistentAgent, job, companyId, 'heartbeat_detected_dead')`.
- In the post-spawn failure handler (Gap 2): in the error path, call `await this.respawnPersistentAgentIfDead(persistentAgent, job, companyId, 'post_spawn_failed')`.
acceptance_tests:
Given a persistent agent whose tmux session is killed
When the heartbeat detects the dead session
Then respawnPersistentAgentIfDead is called with reason=heartbeat_detected_dead
And reloadPersistentAgent is called
And within two heartbeat intervals the session is alive again
And persistent_agents.last_respawn_at is updated in the DB

Given a persistent agent that fails to spawn (post-spawn failure)
When the health check calls respawnPersistentAgentIfDead with reason=post_spawn_failed
Then the respawn is attempted
And the structured log includes role, reason, attempt, and sessionName

Given 3 consecutive respawn failures within RESET_FAILURE_WINDOW_MS (10 min)
When the 4th dead-session event occurs
Then respawnPersistentAgentIfDead does NOT call reloadPersistentAgent
And persistent_agents.status is set to crashed in the DB
And a critical error is logged

Given the circuit breaker window (RESET_FAILURE_WINDOW_MS) has expired since the last failure
When a new dead-session event occurs
Then respawnFailureCount resets to 0
And the respawn is attempted normally

Given two heartbeat ticks fire almost simultaneously when a session is dead
When both ticks call respawnPersistentAgentIfDead concurrently
Then the resetInProgress guard allows only one respawn to proceed
And the second call returns immediately with a log indicating respawn already in progress

Given a healthy persistent agent
When the heartbeat ticks
Then respawnPersistentAgentIfDead is NOT called
And no respawn-related log entries appear
